bottom_line: Standardize review-step prompt commands to use --no-cov for targeted pytest verification so coverage thresholds do not cause false-fail retries.
coverage_notes: DB telemetry available (db-identity check passed). Read prompts and all key run/fix logs for S01-S07 and sampled representative QV/browser logs; no log exceeded 1 MB, so no tail-only truncation strategy was required.

findings.title: Coverage-gated targeted pytest commands caused repeated review-step false failures
findings.recommendation: Update code-review prompt templates to require --no-cov (or an equivalent coverage-safe instruction) for targeted pytest runs used only for behavioral verification.
evidence: ai-dev/logs/I-00086_S04_run15.log:606 — 'ERROR: Coverage failure: total of 29 is less than fail-under=50'
ai-dev/logs/I-00086_S06_run15.log:41 — 'ERROR: Coverage failure: total of 18 is less than fail-under=50'
ai-dev/logs/I-00086_S07_run7.log:55 — 'ERROR: Coverage failure: total of 18 is less than fail-under=50'
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Update CodeReview prompt templates to require --no-cov for targeted pytest verification commands to avoid coverage false-fail retry loops; evidence captured in ai-dev/active/I-00086/reports/I-00086_self_assess_report.md.
